methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $26M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Fuse Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Nuclear Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M+

data.narrative:
Deep Isolation, a Series A nuclear energy company founded by Elizabeth Muller, is evaluated against a sector comprising 3 public comps and 8 private peers including Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power. The company has raised $26M to date.

The Stage-Progression method is the only applicable triangulation approach. It applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement to the $26M total raised proxy, yielding an implied valuation of $52M. The Peer-Median method could not be applied: while 8 nuclear energy peers exist, the narrow filters—same stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier—did not produce the requisite three or more comparable datapoints. Relying on all-sector median would introduce unacceptable noise given the heterogeneity of nuclear technology sub-segments.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $44M (low) to $60M (high), with a median of $52M. This range reflects the applicability constraints: only a single method produced data, and peer comparability remains sparse due to the nascent, fragmented nature of the nuclear technology sector. Key uncertainties include the appropriate stage-progression multiple for this sector—the assumed 2.0x may not reflect Deep Isolation's specific unit economics, tech maturity, or capital requirements relative to frontier nuclear peers—and the absence of sufficient same-stage private comparables to validate the base valuation assumption. Investors should view this estimate as preliminary pending deeper operational due diligence.
